concerns.text: No control for material composition confounds. Different A/B/X elements have intrinsic thermal properties independent of structure. Without controlling for composition (e.g., by stratification or including composition descriptors), observed correlations may reflect compositional effects rather than structural effects.

Thermal conductivity is temperature-dependent but no temperature normalization or measurement condition standardization is specified. Values from different measurement temperatures cannot be directly compared without correction.

Power analysis is post-hoc only. If actual N < 40 (minimum per spec), the study will be underpowered to detect meaningful effects. Consider pre-registering a minimum N threshold or adaptive stopping rule.

5-fold cross-validation without material-family stratification risks data leakage. Similar perovskites (e.g., same A/B cations) in both train and test folds will inflate R². Should stratify by material family or use leave-one-family-out CV.
